mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 9.0 percent within a single month, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 9.0 percent, or the signal predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion, which compresses equity risk premiums and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across three consecutive rolling 5-year windows, or INDPRO declines while HOUST rises consistently yield positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, a contraction in real consumer credit signals tightening financial conditions that override earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises more than 0.25pp in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when FEDFUNDS rises and RSAFS contracts simultaneously, or the interaction term loses statistical significance over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained corporate hiring and revenue growth, which mechanically supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in months with payroll growth are not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 3.5 percent annualized, it triggers a risk-on regime where equity risk premiums compress, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.5 percent, or the signal directional accuracy drops below 55 percent over a 15-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the federal funds rate and consumer price index signals unanchored inflation expectations that force real rates higher, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL increase fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 40 percent of historical instances, or forward returns show no significant difference between high-inflation periods and baseline.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment, a sharp monthly decline in the jobless rate acts as a mean-reversion catalyst that boosts market sentiment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE exceeds 9.5% and PAYEMS rises more than 150k in one month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the signal predictive power disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes over a 10-year walk-forward test.
